description: A user downloaded what they believed was the SysInternals Suite but was actually a malware downloader. The system became slow and unresponsive after execution. The malware modified the hosts file, downloaded a secondary payload, and installed it as a fake Windows service.
tier_note: Soluciones ampliamente publicadas. No usar para scoring; sí para desarrollo.

artifacts.content: File: C:\Users\Public\Downloads\SysInternals.exe | VT hash: 72e6d1728a546c2f3ee32c063ed09fa6ba8c46ac33b0dd2e354087c1ad26ef48 | Spoofed version info: CompanyName='SysInternals, Inc.', FileDescription='SysInternals Suite Downloader', FileVersion='2.0.0.1' | APIs: URLDownloadToFileA, InternetOpenUrlA, ShellExecuteA
forensic_anomalies: The executable has version information mimicking SysInternals (Microsoft) but is not legitimate
Presence of file download APIs (URLDownloadToFileA) indicates a downloader

artifacts.content: UTC Timeline: 2022-11-15 21:16:08 - IEUser logon | 21:17:03 - PowerShell modifies Defender and hosts file | 21:18:40 - SysInternals.exe download via MSEdge | 21:19:00 - Double-click SysInternals.exe | 21:19:17 - Download from http://www.malware430.com/html/VMwareUpdate.exe | 21:19:22 - vmtoolsIO.exe installed as service
forensic_anomalies: Compressed event sequence in 3 minutes: download → execution → secondary download → service installation
Download from malware430.com occurs 17 seconds after execution

artifacts.content: Executable strings: 'c:\Windows\vmtoolsIO.exe' | 'c:\Windows\' | '/C c:\Windows\vmtoolsIO.exe -install && net start VMwareIOHelperService && sc config VMwareIOHelperService start= auto' | Hosts modified: sysinternals.com and malware430.com redirected to 192.168.x.x
forensic_anomalies: The malware installs a service with a VMware-like name to avoid detection
Modification of the hosts file to redirect legitimate domains to internal IPs

artifacts.content: Malicious service installed: Name='VMwareIOHelperService', Executable='c:\Windows\vmtoolsIO.exe', StartType=auto | USN Journal: Creation and deletion of SysInternals.exe | Deleted prefetch: *.pf removed (anti-forensics) | BAM/DAM: SysInternals.exe and conhost.exe executed
forensic_anomalies: Service name mimicking VMware to evade detection
Deletion of prefetch files as an anti-forensic technique
